title: Level 155
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:the.numb.one}} {{Background|file=PHOTO 8 the numb one.jpg}} {{Warning|content=abuse, suicide and dementia}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Bacasime+Antique&family=IM+Fell+DW+Pica:ital@0;1&family=SUSE+Mono:ital,wght@0,100..800;1,100..800&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Expletus+Sans:ital,wght@0,400..700;1,400..700&family=Rubik+Iso&family=Unkempt:wght@400;700&family=Xanh+Mono:ital@0;1&display=swap'); }}
You had wandered for what seemed eons. You had seen lots thrive, as other withered. You had seen creatures devour and maul what you bestowed. You had seen your world crumble once and twice. '''Why had you done that?''' The stretching halls, the M.E.G. agents rapidly chasing you. A dead end. There is no escape. They are a few turns away. Suddenly, the floor breaks and leaves a hole embedded in a mystical light. As you are seen by the men, you have nothing to do but '''fall.''' '''Fall into the eternal light, until you have been cleansed.''' '''But your sins are not to be pardoned.''' '''You fall for eternity, until an proper punishment is attained.''' Ground, once again, you feel your feet. Wind, once again, you feel your body. Something is missing, you cannot tell what. You feel something growing inside you, it grows uncontrollably. As a mycelium inside the carcass of a rotting apple. You look. [[File:PHOTO 1 the numb one .jpg|left|thumb|360x360px]] And you turn yourself. [[File:PHOTO 2 the numb one .jpg|left|thumb|360x360px]] The landscape takes the form of a volcanic desert. Hills, rocks, and dead bushes are all you see. '''All you will ever see.''' You haven’t been here before, nor heard anything about it. You feel forgotten and vast. Left behind in a sea of an abandoned heaven. You feel your soul tremble as a step is taken forward. The ground feels fake. Plastic, concrete, something you are unable to describe. It feels weird. As if you were in a box, a mouse which hasn’t ever seen beyond their cage. Left away, a purgatory. When will the world '''love you once again?''' Unimportant to the whole, no one cares.
You feel === FORGOTTEN === The world alone, in silence. No thoughts to be heard, no thoughts to be spoken. No dangers to be aware from, no reasons to fight. Alone, you drown under the weight of the words you never told. Alone, you drown among your thoughts. What only accompanies you are the rocks. Despaired, you sing to them: all your fears, all your worries, all what eats you from inside. The rocks don’t react, nor hear. As unwillingly being tethered to the floor. Your words rebound over and on as you realize: you are alone in the vastness of the universe. You are alone. You had always been. You will always be. You are hopeless, trying to climb an ever-growing mountain. to reach the top to later fall. Fall to the forgotten.
'''You felt yourself falling endlessly. Infinite, perpetual.''' '''You feel your mind whitening every breath you take.''' '''As the world forgets you, you begin to forget yourself.''' How long have you been wandering? How long was it since you saw someone? How long has it been since you saw '''her?''' To how many rocks have you sang? Singing about fear, dread, ire and love. Happiness, joy, sadness, forgiveness. To how many? [[File:PHOTO 3 the numb one .jpg|left|thumb|360x360px]] Forgiveness, a privilege only awarded to the purest.purest. A sense of comfort as someone is indulged. An ant spared from being stomped, given the opportunity to continue. Forgiveness, cleansing. '''Why weren’t you forgiven?''' You wouldn’t be forgiven, you wouldn’t. All the judging looks of people as you are exiled. They all '''know''', they won’t speak. The landscape '''knows'''. They all '''know'''. A sense of paranoia settles in as you seem to watch the level fluctuate and bend. Ginormous hills appear, the earth cracks endlessly. You gaze the abyss, never-ending darkness. You gaze the mountains, never-ending motion. This place is devoid of anything, you must escape. Devoid of life or understanding. Devoid of love. But had you given '''so much.''' You did it right. You did it right. You did it right. … Deep inside you, you know you didn’t. Yet you won’t admit it. You can’t, she would be '''disappointed.''' '''Yet what could you do?'''
You feel === REMORSEFUL === A parasite eating you from inside. Feeding in the emotional leftovers of conflicts. Your heart beats faster, as you realize '''The Backrooms know it all.''' '''Know all your insecurities, your dreads.''' Your heart pounds even faster. You feel adrenaline rushing through your veins, as if ready to run. Run away from your mind, this place. And end it all once and forever. You try to grab the rocks. Anchored to the floor, they condemn you to suffer the '''consequences.''' You lunge yourself towards the abyss, to meet yourself where you started. You cry to the heavens, to your body, to the world. '''Singing won’t help this time.''' Your heart palpitations rise, your body is at it’s limit. Remorse eats you. How would they know it? You did everything right. There was no way. You collapse in fear and despair.
To wake up again, now in the heights. You seem to be in the top of an endlessly tall mountainous range. The eminences stretch downwards as far as the eye can reach. You look. [[File:PHOTO 4 the numb one.jpg|left|thumb|360x360px]] And you turn yourself. [[File:PHOTO 5 the numb one.jpg|left|thumb|360x360px]] You see some abandoned buildings, stranded in the middle of nowhere. Alone. Forgotten. Silenced. For a brief moment, you see someone '''waving at you.''' '''Someone.''' You rush towards the houses, hoping to find people. Stranded as you are. Willing to talk. As you arrive, you don’t see anyone. So you look for them. Endlessly looking for that person which hid among the houses. You thought you were looking for people. '''The ones above only saw you running in circles.''' You surrender, that person clearly didn’t need you. So you wonder. Why? Aren’t we the same, won’t we be the same? Weren’t we the same? Why are there houses here? You sing to the rocks. The rocks still don’t give response. They still feel fake, surreal. They look unloving. You have wandered for what seemed eons. Yet never have you fallen into dementia. Why now? You wonder. Look at the sky, you, unnecessary. The sky gazes back with disappointment, disapproving. This place feels stranded. You wonder. You wonder how you will escape. You wonder what you’ll do once you escape this place. '''Probably die.''' You won’t survive without '''her.''' You want to see her. So badly. '''You need her.'''
You feel === WHIMSICAL === You want to escape now. You want to see people. Now. Why can’t you get out of this level? Why can’t you get a taste of freedom? I want to escape now, you say to yourself. You want it. You believe you will do so. You want to see her. '''And you will kill whoever gets in your way.''' '''As you have always done.''' You want they to viscerally die. They distanced you from her. But not for too long. You’ll escape. You want to escape. You want to escape. They distanced you. It was not that bad. You wanted it that way, you wanted them to suffer. You want everything. But nothing is given to you. You scream to the heavens. They mockingly laugh at you. They burlesque you, you don’t want that. You want to win this. Even though you know deeply you can’t. That’s why the world laughs at you. That’s why you always want more, why you '''always wanted more.'''
You never were given what you deserved. Since your birth, the world has ever hated you. Until you found her; Blue eyes, nonchalant smile wherever she went. She didn’t care she was inside a cage, she always looked the bright side. '''So you fell for her.''' Finally had the heavens given you something, but you wanted '''more:''' As she went for resources, you feasted in what she attained. She was left with '''nothing.''' But she didn’t scream. She didn’t plead, she just assimilated it. '''That made you love her crazily.''' They found her! [155] —————————————————————— Patient [155] refuses to disclose any information regarding her kidnapper, claiming to “never willing to reveal anything about the You feel ASHAMED You want to disappear. To never be seen again, to never be known about. All the judging looks are just too much. So alone, you want to restart. Reset again and again until you find the perfect run. '''But life only gives you one shot.''' Every look judged you. Since your first breath. They all disapproved. They aren’t looking for you. No one is. The Backrooms feast upon you, '''knowing there won’t ever be consequences.''' A black swan, born to die alone. But maybe you want it that way. You don’t want to be looked at. You don’t want to be judged by. You want to be alone. '''But this hellscape won’t allow that.''' Just run, just run. Run without looking at them. Don’t cry, don’t cry. They won’t care, don’t show weakness. Don’t. Close your eyes. They are not there. They will disappear. Close your eyes and count to ten. '''One.''' Suddenly, silence. Maybe they’re gone. '''Two.''' '''Three.''' You start to hear growling. Those things are getting close. '''Four.''' '''Five.''' '''Six.''' The growling intensifies. You hear them. Don’t open your eyes. '''Seven.''' '''Eight.''' You feel them '''breathing behind you. Run faster. Don’t open your eyes.''' '''nine.''' '''they are around you. don’t open your eyes. don't don't don’t don’t don’t.''' '''ten''' [[File:PHOTO 8 the numb one.jpg|center|thumb|360x360px]] '''They are gone. '''
You breathe calmly as you realize the danger has left. But maybe there was never a danger. Your head hurts badly. Maybe there was never a person. Your heart hurts Forgotten. Remorseful. Whimsical. Ashamed. So alone, stranded. At first you didn’t care. You knew you didn’t need them. '''But now you do.''' Now that they don’t need you anymore. She is alone. '''You abandoned her, you ruined her.''' You made her life more miserable than yet being stuck in the backrooms. '''How long has it been?''' You reflect about all you have done yet. All what you did wrong. '''But what had you done right?''' So alone, you reflect, far away your mind goes. You realize people have tried to be by your side. Yet you didn’t let them, now, forgotten, you seat by the shore. The shore, the sea, the sun. They all accept me. So alone, you reflect, deeply you do. You realize '''what you did was wrong. You never were right.''' Remorseful, you start to drown. Water, lungs, air. Suffocation. So alone, you reflect, alone you are. You realize you don’t deserve freedom. You must stay stranded. Whimsically, water fills your lungs. Your life drying out as saltiness corrodes you. So alone, you reflect, nothing is left. You feel ashamed for your actions, you are sorry for '''her.''' Ashamed, you drown. As your eyes close off, you aren’t judged anymore. Now,
You feel NUMB Nothing is left. You have lost everything what you have ever gotten. Your world has crumbled again. Yet you are unable remember anything. The world black, lights gone. Sensations left back, you are no more. You cease to be. What are you now? From above, they look. They judge. They know. They know they are right. What do you feel? What do you see? What do you love? What do you fear? Hold tight onto it. Your world is gone. Alone. Your conscience is what only is left. Hold tight onto it. You can’t feel. You can’t be. Nothingness, the void and the darkness. Alone for eternity. You finally are numb.
'''OPEN AUTHOR AND LICENSING INFORMATION'''
